title: Memory Conflict Resolution
constraints: When a fact is updated, the old version must be replaced, not duplicated
The agent must use the most recent value when a conflict exists
The update logic must handle arbitrary fact types, not just hardcoded keys
The agent should never contradict itself within a single response
hints: Store facts as key-value pairs where the key identifies the fact category (e.g., 'location', 'job')
Before inserting a new fact, check if a fact with the same key already exists and overwrite it
Use the LLM to extract structured key-value pairs from user statements so you can detect conflicts automatically

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Facts are appended without checking for conflicts or updates
memory_store = []

def add_fact(fact: str):
    memory_store.append(fact)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    add_fact(f"User said: {user_input}")
    facts_block = "\n".join(memory_store)
    messages = [
        SystemMessage(content=f"You are a helpful assistant. Known facts:\n{facts_block}"),
        HumanMessage(content=user_input),
    ]
    response = llm.invoke(messages)
    return response.content

# Turn 1: User states location
print(chat("I live in New York City."))

# Turn 2: User updates location
print(chat("Actually, I just moved to Los Angeles."))

# Turn 3: Where does the user live? Agent sees BOTH facts and may contradict itself
print(chat("Where do I live?"))

print(f"\nStored facts: {memory_store}")
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

# BUG: Facts are appended without checking for conflicts or updates
memory_store = []

def add_fact(fact: str):
    memory_store.append(fact)

assistant = Agent(
    role="Assistant",
    goal="Remember and recall facts about the user",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant who remembers what users tell you.",
)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    add_fact(f"User said: {user_input}")
    facts_block = "\n".join(memory_store)
    task = Task(
        description=f"Known facts:\n{facts_block}\n\nRespond to: {user_input}",
        expected_output="A helpful response using known facts",
        agent=assistant,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
    return str(crew.kickoff())

print(chat("I live in New York City."))
print(chat("Actually, I just moved to Los Angeles."))
print(chat("Where do I live?"))

print(f"\nStored facts: {memory_store}")
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Facts are appended without checking for conflicts or updates
memory_store = []

def add_fact(fact: str):
    memory_store.append(fact)

agent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant who remembers what users tell you.",
)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    add_fact(f"User said: {user_input}")
    facts_block = "\n".join(memory_store)
    prompt = f"Known facts:\n{facts_block}\n\nRespond to: {user_input}"
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, prompt)
    return result.final_output

print(chat("I live in New York City."))
print(chat("Actually, I just moved to Los Angeles."))
print(chat("Where do I live?"))

print(f"\nStored facts: {memory_store}")

evaluationCriteria.label: Updates replace old facts
evaluationCriteria.description: When a user corrects a fact, the old value is replaced rather than both coexisting

evaluationCriteria.label: Uses the latest value
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent always uses the most recently stated value when answering

evaluationCriteria.label: No self-contradictions
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent never states conflicting information within a single response
